articles.title: ANTHROPIC PARTNERS WITH U.S. NATIONAL LABS
articles.summary: Anthropic has joined the U.S. Department of Energy's 1,000 Scientist AI Jam, where frontier AI models, including Claude 3.7 Sonnet, will be tested on scientific and national security challenges.

articles.title: DEEPSEEK RELEASES REVENUE INFORMATION
articles.summary: DeepSeek released its inference and revenue numbers at the end of its open source week. The company offers many services for free - if it monetized every token, it would make $200m or so in yearly revenue with healthy margins.

articles.title: INCEPTION EMERGES FROM STEALTH WITH A NEW TYPE OF AI MODEL
articles.summary: Inception, a Palo Alto startup, has developed a diffusion-based large language model, DLM, which significantly accelerates AI text generation compared to traditional LLMs. Founded by Stanford's Stefano Ermon, the company claims its models are faster, more cost-effective, and offer up to 10x improved performance. It has secured investment from Mayfield Fund and attracted several Fortune 100 customers, offering various deployment options and an API.

articles.title: ROBUST RLHF WITH PREFERENCE AS REWARD
articles.summary: A systematic study on reward shaping in RLHF led to Preference As Reward (PAR), a method that uses latent preferences to enhance alignment, improve data efficiency, and mitigate reward hacking, outperforming existing approaches on multiple benchmarks.

articles.title: HVI COLOR SPACE
articles.summary: A new color space, Horizontal/Vertical-Intensity (HVI), along with the CIDNet model, significantly reduces color artifacts and improves low-light image enhancement.

articles.title: ENHANCED MULTIMODAL CORRESPONDENCE
articles.summary: ReCon introduces a dual-alignment learning framework to improve multimodal correspondence accuracy by enforcing cross-modal and intra-modal relation consistency.

articles.title: MOST ADS GET IGNORED—HERE'S WHERE THEY DON'T
articles.summary: Tech pros scroll past ads all day—but not here. TLDR reaches millions of devs, founders, and decision makers who actually open, read, and engage with what's inside. And to make things easier, TLDR's team writes the ad copy for you, for free. Learn more [10] about running your first ad campaign.

articles.title: UNDERSTANDING DIFFUSION PLANNING
articles.summary: A large-scale empirical study of diffusion planning in offline reinforcement learning.

articles.title: MODEL PRE-TRAINING ON LIMITED RESOURCES
articles.summary: Through a benchmark using various academic GPUs, this study demonstrates that models like Pythia-1B can be pre-trained in far fewer GPU-days than traditionally required.

articles.title: SPEECH ENHANCEMENT WITH VOICERESTORE
articles.summary: VoiceRestore is an advanced tool for restoring and enhancing speech recordings using deep learning aimed at improving clarity and removing noise.

articles.title: ANTHROPIC USED POKÉMON TO BENCHMARK ITS NEWEST AI MODEL
articles.summary: Anthropic tested its Claude 3.7 Sonnet AI model by having it play Pokémon Red, showcasing its "extended thinking" capabilities. The model successfully battled three gym leaders, significantly outperforming its predecessor, Claude 3.0 Sonnet. While details on computing power and time required are unclear, the test highlighted games as a tool for AI benchmarking.

articles.title: NOTAGEN SHEET MUSIC GENERATION
articles.summary: By training an auto-regressive model to generate sheet music, this team has been able to make a novel text-to-music system that is often preferred by human raters.

articles.title: PERPLEXITY WANTS TO REINVENT THE WEB BROWSER WITH AI
articles.summary: Perplexity has announced its new browser, Comet, which is aimed at competing with Google's Chrome. No details on its features are available yet. The company has invited users to sign up for beta access in a bid to gain early adopters. Perplexity's move indicates a growing trend of AI-specific applications challenging traditional app categories.

articles.title: WARP LAUNCHES AI-FIRST TERMINAL APP FOR WINDOWS
articles.summary: Sam Altman-backed Warp is reimagining the command-line terminal after nearly 40 years unchanged.

articles.title: UV AND RAY IN CLUSTERS
articles.summary: Ray now has first-class support for automatic dependence installation based on Python package management uv.

articles.title: PRIME INTELLECT RAISES $15M
articles.summary: Prime Intellect, a distributed computing company, has raised additional funds to continue pushing its distributed training scheme.
